9271	\begin{lemma}[Pushforward along an iso commutes with the free functor]
9272	  \label{lem:pushforward_commutes_free}
9273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_commutes_free}
9274	  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
9275	  \uses{def:jshriek_ou}
9276	  Let \(\varphi : X \cong Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes and write
9277	  \(\Phi = \operatorname{pushforwardEquivOfIso}\varphi : X.\mathrm{Modules} \simeq Y.\mathrm{Modules}\)
9278	  for the induced equivalence of module categories, with \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\) the corresponding
9279	  pushforward on presheaves of modules along the homeomorphism underlying \(\varphi\). Then for every
9280	  presheaf of modules \(P\) on \(X\) there is a natural isomorphism
9281	  \[
9282	    \Phi.\mathrm{functor}.\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\operatorname{free} P\bigr)
9283	      \;\cong\;
9284	    \operatorname{free}\bigl(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}} P\bigr),
9285	  \]

9287	\end{lemma}
9288	\begin{proof}
9289	  \uses{def:jshriek_ou}

9291	  presheaves of abelian groups, and pushforward along the isomorphism \(\varphi\) is itself an
9292	  equivalence that commutes with the forgetful functors --- it relabels opens along the underlying
9293	  homeomorphism and leaves the underlying abelian-group data untouched. A left adjoint composed with an
9294	  equivalence is again a left adjoint, now to the conjugate forgetful functor; the mate of the identity
9295	  comparison between the two forgetful functors is the asserted natural isomorphism
9296	  \(\Phi(\operatorname{free} P) \cong \operatorname{free}(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}} P)\), canonical by
9297	  uniqueness of left adjoints.

9324	\begin{lemma}[The representable transports across the homeomorphism]
9325	  \label{lem:yoneda_transport_along_homeo}
9326	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.yoneda_transport_along_homeo}
9327	  % NOTE: build target. The Lean declaration does not exist yet.
9328	  For a scheme isomorphism \(\varphi : X \cong Y\), whose underlying homeomorphism induces the
9329	  order-isomorphism of frames \(\operatorname{Opens} X \cong \operatorname{Opens} Y\), and any open
9330	  \(V \subseteq X\), the presheaf pushforward of the representable presheaf carries it to the
9331	  representable on the image open:
9332	  \[
9333	    \Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\, V\bigr) \;\cong\; \mathbf{y}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{hom} \,{''}\, V),
9334	  \]
9335	  where \(\varphi.\mathrm{hom} \,{''}\, V\) is the image open of \(V\) in \(Y\).
9336	\end{lemma}
9337	\begin{proof}
9338	  The presheaf pushforward \(\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\) acts on opens by the inverse-image order-isomorphism
9339	  \(W \mapsto \varphi^{-1}(W)\) of the homeomorphism. Hence
9340	  \((\Phi_{\mathrm{pre}}\,\mathbf{y}\,V)(W) = (\mathbf{y}\,V)(\varphi^{-1} W)\) is inhabited exactly when
9341	  \(\varphi^{-1} W \subseteq V\), i.e.\ when \(W \subseteq \varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V\); this is
9342	  precisely \((\mathbf{y}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{hom}\,{''}\,V))(W)\). The bijection is natural in \(W\)
9343	  because the open-image map is monotone and inverts the restriction order, so the two representables
9344	  coincide as presheaves.
9345	\end{proof}
